steps.title: Contrast crowd and comment
steps.content: The crowd is clearly not united: some people shout Bread, some shout Taxes, and no one seems to know what they want. The Lord Chancellor nevertheless describes the shouting as having unanimity.

steps.title: Match the contradiction
steps.content: Choice A says he claims to know what the crowd wants, but he repeatedly asks what the shouting means. Choice B says he wants to speak to the crowd, which is not stated. Choice C says he sympathizes with the crowd and is annoyed, which the text does not show. The remaining choice captures his mistaken description of unity. The answer is D.
